summary: Split from MPI-194 live testing: long-video LTX stage-gap SCALES with frames (short ~9s, 5s=45s, 10s=111s) despite the 42GB transformer staged on disk. 10s gen VRAM 17/32GB + RAM 66/92GB = aimdo VRAM<->RAM offload churn, NOT the MPI-191 volume tax. User probing 30s for OOM. Next: local repro + stock-template repro + pod-log classify. Distinct root cause; MPI-191/194 volume finding stays proven.

★ 30s data point = SUPER-LINEAR, rules out weight-read-bound. Full curve: short ~9s, 5s 45s, 10s 111s, 30s 620s (10min20s). Linear-from-10s would predict ~333s for 30s; actual 620s = ~1.9x worse. Math: full 51GB weight set from volume = only 68s; 42GB transformer from disk = 42s. 620s is ~9x any single/few weight re-read => NOT I/O-bound on weights. Points to aimdo THRASHING (repeated evict/re-fault cycles) as it juggles a working set >> 32GB VRAM on the 30s latent. Stage-1 itself = 4m30s (sampling, scales w/ frames, expected). Stage-1 VRAM stable 22.7/32, RAM 58.8/92 during sampling. CONCLUSION forming: long-video gap is aimdo dynamic-VRAM management on a 32GB card, NOT a slow-tier read -> hot-store/disk-staging CANNOT fix it. Real levers: bigger-VRAM card (48/80GB), --reserve-vram, tiled/chunked decode. Confirms MPI-197 offload-churn hypothesis over volume-tax. PENDING: did 30s survive to stage-2 or OOM? RAM peak during the 620s gap?

30s COMPLETED on 32GB (no OOM; VRAM->1.8 RAM->63.9 idle after). Full 20m52s breakdown: stage-1 4m30s + GAP 10m20s(620s) + stage-2 5m52s. THE GAP IS ~HALF THE TOTAL RUNTIME. Super-linear gap curve confirmed: 9s/45s/111s/620s for short/5s/10s/30s. Gap is aimdo evict/re-fault thrash on a working set >>32GB VRAM, NOT slow-tier I/O (620s >> 68s full-weight-set-from-volume) -> hot-store/disk-staging cannot help. SECOND finding (product, not perf): 30s output QUALITY is mediocre — wavy morphing + artifacts. Distilled/low-step LTX pipeline loses coherence at 30s; LTX 30s marketing assumes full model + 30-40 steps, not our fast tiers. => PRODUCT DECISION likely needed: cap video length or warn past N seconds, rather than chase perf on a length that also looks bad. MPI-197 reframed: (a) confirm aimdo-thrash via local + stock-template repro; (b) decide product length policy (cap/warn) given BOTH the 620s gap AND quality degradation. Levers if pursuing perf: bigger-VRAM card, --reserve-vram, tiled decode.

SAMPLER A/B (local, 3s video). CURRENT stage-2 sampler: 32s to START stage-2 (dead-gap) + 22s sampling = 54s total. LTX sampler: 15s to first step (front-loads step 1, no big dead-gap) + 25s remaining steps = 40s total. LTX ~14s faster locally BUT the shape differs crucially: current sampler pays a 32s UPFRONT GAP (evict->re-fault->reload before step 1) then fast sampling; LTX rolls straight into step 1 with no evict-everything moment. HYPOTHESIS: the current sampler dead-gap IS what thrashes aimdo super-linearly on the pod (32s local 3s -> 620s pod 30s). If LTX sampler has no big boundary gap, it may AVOID the pod blowup entirely -> could be the real MPI-197 lever + cheaper than a card-tier chase. Even locally there is a 32s gap on a 3s video, so the boundary cost is partly WORKFLOW-inherent, not purely pod/aimdo. OPEN + decisive: (1) QUALITY of LTX sampler vs current (morphing/artifacts better/same/worse?) — faster is moot if quality drops; (2) does LTX sampler kill the GAP on the POD at 10s/30s? Next: swap sampler in stage-2 workflow, A/B quality local, then pod re-test the gap curve.

SAMPLER SWAP = DEAD END (user-confirmed). 5s local A/B: NORMAL sampler 17s load + 30s sampling = 47s; LTX sampler 19s load + (16s step1 + 14s steps2-3 = 30s) = 49s. Same load, same sampling, same total, SAME QUALITY. The LTX samplers front-loads-step-1 was just a different progress BREAKDOWN, not less work — no gap advantage. Sampler choice does NOT touch the stage-boundary cost. => the pod 620s long-video gap is confirmed aimdo VRAM<->RAM thrash on 32GB, unfixable by workflow/sampler. Remaining MPI-197 levers are the expensive/structural ones ONLY: bigger-VRAM card (48/80GB so working set fits, no thrash), --reserve-vram tuning, tiled/chunked decode, OR the PRODUCT call = cap/warn video length past ~5-10s (also justified by 30s quality degradation). Local-repro done tonight (pod deleted, no more pod tests today). Next session: stock-template pod repro to confirm aimdo-inherent, then product length-policy decision.
